seoTitle: How to Create a Poll in Typeform
metaDescription: Learn how to build an interactive poll in Typeform, customize the design, share it online, and analyze responses in real time.
intro: Polls are a quick way to gather opinions or engage an audience without the overhead of a full survey. This guide covers how to create a poll in Typeform, add answer choices, customize the design, and analyze results as they come in.

steps.heading: Create a new poll
steps.body: Start a new Typeform designed for a poll, focusing on a simple question or a short set of choices.

steps.heading: Add questions and answer choices
steps.body: Enter your poll question and the answer options you want respondents to choose from.

steps.heading: Customize and brand your poll
steps.body: Adjust the design, colors, and branding to keep the poll consistent with your other content.

steps.heading: Share and analyze responses
steps.body: Publish and share the poll link, then review responses in real time using Typeform's results tools.

faq.question: Can I see poll results as they come in?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform lets you view and analyze responses in real time as people submit the poll.

faq.question: What's the difference between a poll and a survey in Typeform?
faq.answer: A poll is typically a shorter form focused on one or a few questions for quick opinions, while a survey usually covers more questions in greater depth.

faq.question: Can I customize the design of my poll?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust themes, colors, and branding to match your organization's style before sharing the poll.

faq.question: Is Typeform good for event polling?
faq.answer: Yes, polls built in Typeform work well for event organizers, educators, and marketers looking for quick audience engagement.
